description: All structural framing is complete, inspected, and certified by Baldwin County before MEP rough-in begins.

categories.name: Crew & Schedule
items: Framing crew lead confirmed on-site with current certificate of insurance on file
Framing schedule posted and distributed — wall framing, roof framing, and sheathing milestones dated
Lumber package delivery confirmed received on-site, quantities verified against takeoff, shortages flagged to supplier
Windows and exterior doors confirmed staged or scheduled for delivery to align with rough opening completion
Superintendent site visit scheduled at sole plate, wall, and roof-deck milestones before crew advances

categories.name: Materials On-Site
items: All framing lumber graded, stamped, and dry (moisture content ≤19% per IRC R502.1 / R802.1); wet sticks flagged and pulled
Engineered lumber (LVL beams, TJI joists, PSL columns) on-site, dry-stacked off ground, mill certs on file
Hurricane straps, H-clips, joist hangers, and all Simpson or equivalent connectors on-site and match structural engineering schedule
Sheathing (OSB or plywood) on-site, rated per structural drawings; exposure rating confirmed for Baldwin County coastal wind zone
Sheathing fasteners confirmed — nail size, spacing, and pattern per engineer's shear-wall schedule
Sill seal and pressure-treated sole plates on-site for all slab-bearing walls; PT stamp verified

categories.name: Foundation Tie-In
items: Slab or foundation certified clean, level, and dimensionally verified before any wall framing begins
Anchor bolt layout verified against engineer's anchor schedule — bolt diameter, embedment, and spacing spot-checked by superintendent
Pressure-treated sole plates installed, anchor bolts engaged with correct plate washers and nuts, torqued per engineer's spec
Sill seal installed continuously under all sole plates bearing on concrete
Foundation perimeter dimensions confirmed against permitted plans (diagonal measurements within ¼" per 25 LF)

categories.name: Structural Framing
items: All exterior walls framed to plan dimensions, plumb (≤⅛" per 8 LF), straight, and squared before sheathing applied
All interior load-bearing walls located per structural drawings, correct stud size and spacing confirmed
All headers sized per structural schedule; double top plates lapped at corners and intersections per IRC R602.3.2
All LVL beams, PSL columns, and point loads installed per engineer's drawings; bearing seats and post caps verified
Floor system: TJI or lumber joists sized, spaced, and blocked per structural drawings; rim board continuous
Second-floor and ceiling load paths traced from ridge to foundation — no unresolved load-path gaps before sheathing
All stair rough openings framed to plan dimensions; trimmer and header counts verified

categories.name: Rough Openings & Window/Door Prep
items: All window rough openings sized per window schedule (width and height verified against approved shop drawings)
All exterior door rough openings sized per door schedule; king studs, trimmers, and headers count confirmed
Garage door rough openings confirmed to plan width and height; structural header and bearing verified
All rough openings plumb, square, and level (diagonal tolerance ≤⅛"); sill and jack stud condition verified
Window and door bucks or flashings blocked and ready for WRB and flashing installation

categories.name: Roof Framing
items: Roof framing method (stick, truss, or hybrid) matches structural drawings; truss engineering shop drawings on-site and approved
Ridge board or ridge beam sized and installed per structural drawings; ridge height verified against architectural plans
Rafter or truss spacing confirmed (typically 16" o.c.) and consistent across entire roof plane
All hurricane clips and rafter ties installed at every rafter-to-top-plate connection per engineer's connector schedule
Hip, valley, and jack rafter cuts verified for correct plumb and birdsmouth; no excessive notching of structural members
Roof sheathing installed, nailing pattern per shear schedule, H-clips at unsupported edges; no delaminated panels
Roof deck verified flat within ¼" per 10 LF at ridge and eave lines before roofing crew mobilizes

categories.name: Sheathing & Weather Barrier
items: All exterior wall sheathing installed with correct nail size and spacing per engineer's shear-wall schedule; nailing pattern field-verified by superintendent
House wrap or WRB (weather-resistant barrier) installed continuously over sheathing, lapped shingle-fashion (top over bottom), taped at seams per manufacturer spec
All window and door rough openings flashed with self-adhering flashing tape per manufacturer's installation sequence before windows are set
WRB penetrations (hose bibs, electrical, HVAC) sealed and flashed
No tears, gaps, or unsealed laps in WRB; superintendent walkthrough sign-off before any cladding begins

categories.name: MEP Rough-In Prep
items: All plumbing chase walls, wet-wall locations, and drain-waste-vent rough-in paths confirmed with plumbing sub before top-out
HVAC duct chase locations, air-handler platforms, and attic access framed per mechanical drawings
Electrical panel location framed and backing installed; sub-panel locations confirmed
Fire-blocking installed at all required locations per IRC R302.11 — top and bottom plates at floor levels, around penetrations, at soffits
Backing for future fixtures confirmed blocked in — shower valves, grab bars, TV walls, heavy light fixtures, range hoods

categories.name: Inspections
items: Baldwin County framing inspection scheduled and passed — inspection card signed and posted on-site
Baldwin County wind mitigation inspection (if required for coastal insurance compliance) completed and documentation secured
Structural engineer or third-party inspector walk completed if required by project specs or lender; written sign-off on file
Superintendent final framing punch walk completed; all open items resolved before MEP subs mobilize
Superintendent photo documentation of framing, anchor bolts, connector hardware, and sheathing nailing uploaded to project Drive folder

categories.name: Quality & Sign-Off
items: No cracked, split, or notched structural members left in place; all field repairs reviewed and approved by superintendent
All doubled joists, beams, and columns glued and nailed per structural requirements; no dry stacking of engineered lumber
All temporary bracing removed after structure is self-supporting and sheathed
Attic access framed and sized per IRC M1305.1.3 (minimum 22"×30" opening); pull-down or hatch type confirmed against plans
Framing phase completion confirmed in writing by superintendent; green light issued to MEP subs to begin rough-in
